regimens.key_clue: ACS/MI/stable CAD/angina/revascularization/ischemic stroke/TIA/PAD/atherosclerotic aortic disease
regimens.manual_review_note: Secondary prevention; should not be treated as simple screening.

regimens.key_clue: Worsening wheeze/SOB/chest tightness/cough, increased reliever need
regimens.manual_review_note: Use as triage/safety row; severe exacerbation should not be OPD-only.

regimens.key_clue: Asthma symptoms <3 times/week and lung function >80%
regimens.manual_review_note: Add ICS inhaler products before allowing RX NOW guideline-complete.

regimens.key_clue: Episodic cough/wheeze/dyspnea/chest tightness; worse night/early morning; triggered by allergen/cold/exercise/viral infection
regimens.manual_review_note: Workbook lacks inhaled ICS asthma controller products.

regimens.display_name: Abnormal uterine bleeding triage / investigation gate
regimens.disease_name: Abnormal uterine bleeding triage / investigation gate
regimens.key_clue: Bleeding pattern abnormal: interval, duration, amount, regularity; reproductive-age/adolescent
regimens.manual_review_note: Investigation-first row.

regimens.key_clue: Longstanding yellow discharge, burning/itch, vaginal erythema, pH >4.5, high WBC, lactobacilli reduced
regimens.manual_review_note: Source says antibiotic choice not settled; do not default.

regimens.display_name: Known active axial spondyloarthritis requiring target therapy
regimens.disease_name: Known active axial spondyloarthritis requiring target therapy
regimens.key_clue: Known axSpA with active disease despite standard therapy; BASDAI ≥4 or ASDAS ≥2.1
regimens.manual_review_note: Biologics/JAKi not found in workbook as OPD-ready products; keep blocked/specialist-only.

regimens.display_name: Suspected axial spondyloarthritis / inflammatory back pain
regimens.disease_name: Suspected axial spondyloarthritis / inflammatory back pain
regimens.key_clue: Chronic inflammatory back pain, young onset, morning stiffness, improves with exercise, buttock/SI pain, uveitis/psoriasis/IBD history
regimens.manual_review_note: Workbook supports NSAIDs only; biologic/tsDMARD therapy is specialist gate.

regimens.key_clue: Known/suspected Barrett’s on endoscopy; not a simple symptom-only diagnosis
regimens.manual_review_note: BO definition in source: visible columnar lining ≥1 cm above GEJ confirmed by histology; IM not mandatory in Asia-Pacific consensus.

regimens.key_clue: Acute unilateral LMN facial weakness, onset <72h
regimens.manual_review_note: Add oral steroid product/BDS before enabling complete RX NOW; do not use antiviral monotherapy

regimens.key_clue: Child with acute unilateral facial weakness
regimens.manual_review_note: Pediatric steroid evidence inconclusive; no exact pediatric steroid dose extracted

regimens.key_clue: Brief spinning vertigo <1 min triggered by lying down, rolling in bed, looking up, bending down; no new hearing loss/neuro deficit
regimens.manual_review_note: Medication is not primary BPPV therapy; dimenhydrinate only for short rescue/prophylaxis in severe nausea/vertigo around CRP.

regimens.key_clue: Fishy odor, thin homogeneous gray-white discharge, pH >4.5, clue cells/positive whiff
regimens.manual_review_note: Metronidazole 500 mg not found; guideline permits 400–500 mg BID.

regimens.key_clue: Acute cough; normal vitals/lung exam; colored sputum alone is not bacterial
regimens.use_when: Uncomplicated adult acute bronchitis after pneumonia red flags excluded
regimens.manual_review_note: liver toxicity if overdose

regimens.key_clue: Fever/cough/rhinorrhea/nasal congestion/sore throat/headache/myalgia; viral pattern
regimens.use_when: Simple adult viral URI/common cold without bacterial complication
regimens.manual_review_note: liver toxicity if overdose

regimens.key_clue: Dysuria + frequency/urgency small volumes; healthy nonpregnant premenopausal female
regimens.use_when: Use only for uncomplicated lower UTI; exclude pyelo, pregnancy, male, catheter, stone, recurrent/complicated UTI
regimens.manual_review_note: rash, GI upset, SJS rare, photosensitivity

regimens.display_name: Group A streptococcal pharyngitis / streptococcal tonsillitis
regimens.disease_name: Group A streptococcal pharyngitis / streptococcal tonsillitis
regimens.key_clue: Sudden sore throat + fever + odynophagia + tender anterior cervical nodes/exudate; usually no cough/rhinorrhea/hoarseness/oral ulcers/conjunctivitis
regimens.use_when: Use only if RADT or throat culture positive; if clear viral symptoms, route to viral pharyngitis and no GAS testing
regimens.manual_review_note: diarrhea, rash

regimens.key_clue: Age 5–15 yr common; fever, sudden sore throat, painful swallowing, anterior cervical nodes, palatal petechiae/exudate; viral symptoms absent
regimens.use_when: Use only if RADT/culture positive; child >3 yr with negative RADT needs throat culture backup before antibiotics
regimens.manual_review_note: diarrhea, rash

regimens.key_clue: Fever, tonsillar exudate, tender anterior cervical nodes, absence of cough; RADT positive preferred
regimens.use_when: Use only when GAS is confirmed/strongly likely; viral sore throat should route to viral pharyngitis
regimens.manual_review_note: diarrhea, rash

regimens.key_clue: Painful genital vesicles/ulcers or first recognized genital HSV episode
regimens.use_when: Use for suspected/confirmed first episode genital HSV; arrange STI testing/counseling
regimens.manual_review_note: nausea, headache

regimens.key_clue: Recurrent genital HSV prodrome/early lesion; patient recognizes outbreak
regimens.use_when: Use only for recurrent HSV; best if started within 1 day of lesion onset/prodrome
regimens.manual_review_note: nausea, headache

regimens.key_clue: Severe fever/purulent discharge/facial pain >3–4 d OR persistent >10 d OR worsening after URI improvement
regimens.use_when: Use only when CDC bacterial sinusitis criteria are met; otherwise route to viral URI/common cold
regimens.manual_review_note: diarrhea, nausea, rash

regimens.key_clue: Ear fullness / blocked ear / hearing loss with wax seen
regimens.manual_review_note: Treat only symptomatic/obstructing wax or wax preventing exam; do not treat asymptomatic wax with adequate exam

regimens.key_clue: Child >6 months with obstructing wax, hearing concern, fever/ear exam blocked, or speech concern
regimens.manual_review_note: Guideline target patient is >6 months; avoid home/self-irrigation in young child or high-risk ear

regimens.display_name: Confirmed adult OSA with significant comorbidity
regimens.disease_name: Confirmed adult OSA with significant comorbidity
regimens.key_clue: Positive sleep test + heart failure/CAD/arrhythmia/refractory HTN/pulmonary HTN/COPD/stroke/renal failure/DM/depression/neuromuscular disease/opioid use
regimens.manual_review_note: Comorbidity gates should bias toward PAP-first and specialist-supervised testing/titration.

regimens.display_name: Confirmed adult OSA without significant comorbidity
regimens.disease_name: Confirmed adult OSA without significant comorbidity
regimens.key_clue: Positive sleep test; no major cardiopulmonary/neuromuscular/renal/stroke risk
regimens.manual_review_note: Device/procedure row. No routine medication treats OSA itself.

regimens.key_clue: New/progressive constipation with warning signs suggesting organic disease
regimens.manual_review_note: Do not mask alarm constipation with repeated laxatives only.

regimens.key_clue: Hard stool/straining/incomplete evacuation or <3 bowel movements/week, chronic ≥3 months; mild or intermittent
regimens.manual_review_note: Use fiber only with adequate water; may worsen bloating.

regimens.key_clue: Frequent hard stool/straining/incomplete evacuation; persistent symptoms affecting QoL
regimens.manual_review_note: PEG has strongest evidence among conventional osmotics; guideline says conventional laxatives before new agents.

regimens.key_clue: Needs contraception with obesity, VTE, HIV, or valvular heart disease
regimens.manual_review_note: Contraception products incomplete in workbook; gate only.

regimens.key_clue: Recurrent wheals/angioedema ≥2 times/week for >6 weeks, usually no clear cause
regimens.use_when: Chronic urticaria without anaphylaxis; basic labs/trigger review needed
regimens.manual_review_note: drowsy, dry mouth

regimens.key_clue: Mucopurulent discharge, dysuria/urethritis, high PMN, Gram stain negative, NAAT CT positive or CT risk
regimens.manual_review_note: Azithromycin failure higher than doxy in some settings; doxy default if not pregnant.

regimens.key_clue: Burning/irritation, dysuria, yellow discharge without odor, acidic pH, abundant lactobacilli, yeast negative
regimens.manual_review_note: No workbook vaginal sodium bicarbonate product.

regimens.key_clue: Obstructed defecation, excessive straining, manual maneuvers, sensation of blockage; poor laxative response
regimens.manual_review_note: Guideline favors anorectal physiologic testing first in refractory constipation; biofeedback superior for defecation disorder.

regimens.display_name: Type 2 diabetes with chronic kidney disease
regimens.disease_name: Type 2 diabetes with chronic kidney disease
regimens.key_clue: T2DM with reduced eGFR or albuminuria
regimens.manual_review_note: Requires lab-based dosing; not automatic OPD.

regimens.key_clue: Diabetes age ≥40, or younger with risk factors; no clinical ASCVD
regimens.manual_review_note: Ezetimibe missing; needs lab-based target follow-up.

regimens.key_clue: Diabetes patient with low sugar symptoms or glucose ≤70 mg/dL
regimens.manual_review_note: Workbook lacks dextrose/glucagon product rows; keep emergency meds manual.

regimens.key_clue: Risk factor, high sugar, polyuria/polydipsia/weight loss, health check abnormal glucose
regimens.manual_review_note: Do not auto-prescribe from a single unconfirmed asymptomatic reading.

regimens.key_clue: Fever + rash 1-8 weeks after new drug; facial edema, lymphadenopathy, eosinophilia/atypical lymphocyte, hepatitis/renal/lung/heart involvement
regimens.use_when: Suspected severe drug eruption; especially aromatic anticonvulsants, sulfonamides, allopurinol, antibiotics, NSAIDs
regimens.manual_review_note: Prednisolone/cyclosporine missing; avoid auto-RX systemic steroid without labs.

regimens.display_name: Dyslipidemia screening and ASCVD risk assessment
regimens.disease_name: Dyslipidemia screening and ASCVD risk assessment
regimens.key_clue: Age ≥35, ASCVD/subclinical ASCVD, diabetes, CKD, suspected familial dyslipidemia, xanthoma/arcus young
regimens.manual_review_note: Screening/triage row; no drug unless risk group matched.

regimens.key_clue: Ovarian cyst suspicious for endometrioma; cyclic pain or infertility
regimens.manual_review_note: Do not auto-treat large/uncertain cyst as simple dysmenorrhea.

regimens.key_clue: Dysmenorrhea impairing life, chronic pelvic pain, deep dyspareunia, cyclic bowel/urinary pain, infertility with pain
regimens.manual_review_note: Hormonal options recommended by guideline, but several key products missing from workbook.

regimens.key_clue: After conservative surgery for endometriosis/endometrioma; does not currently desire pregnancy
regimens.manual_review_note: Guideline supports OCP/progestogen/GnRHa/LNG-IUS; workbook has COCs/Slinda but not many endometriosis-specific agents.

regimens.key_clue: active anterior nosebleed / recurrent bleeding
regimens.use_when: minor anterior epistaxis after initial assessment; hemodynamically stable
regimens.avoid_when: posterior bleed, unstable vitals, severe trauma, anticoagulant complication, bleeding disorder, persistent heavy bleeding
regimens.red_flags_one_line: ER now if unstable, posterior bleed suspected, major trauma, severe/recurrent bleeding, or cannot stop with compression
regimens.notes_internal: ADD NEW: Thai epistaxis guideline 2024 is procedural; this row is for clinic support/triage, not routine self-medication.

regimens.display_name: Febrile UTI suspected from fever workup
regimens.disease_name: Febrile UTI suspected from fever workup
regimens.key_clue: Fever without source + abnormal UA: leukocyte esterase/nitrite positive or urine WBC ≥5/HPF
regimens.use_when: Use when FWS workup suggests UTI; culture before antibiotic
regimens.verification_status: Thai Pediatric Acute Febrile Illness CPG 2019 + workbook
regimens.manual_review_note: SOURCE_VERIFIED_PARTIAL_PRODUCT_GAP
regimens.source_file: Needs eval/admit if age <3 mo, toxic, vomiting/dehydration, persistent fever, flank pain, bacteremia, or unable oral intake. Do not diagnose UTI from bag culture alone.

regimens.display_name: Suspected fecal impaction / rectal stool burden
regimens.disease_name: Suspected fecal impaction / rectal stool burden
regimens.key_clue: No stool several days + rectal fullness/tenesmus; possible hard stool in rectum; no acute abdomen
regimens.manual_review_note: Do not recommend self large-volume colonic irrigation. Sodium phosphate enema caution in elderly/renal/heart/neuro disease.

regimens.key_clue: Heartburn symptoms with normal endoscopy/reflux burden or poor PPI response; often hypersensitivity/functional overlap
regimens.manual_review_note: Do not present as default GERD row; use after refractory assessment.

regimens.key_clue: Rectal/core temp >38°C, no clear source, age 0-28 days
regimens.use_when: Any fever without clear source in neonate; also premature infant corrected early period
regimens.verification_status: Thai Pediatric Acute Febrile Illness CPG 2019
regimens.manual_review_note: SOURCE_VERIFIED_ER_GATE
regimens.source_file: Cannot routine OPD. ER/admit now + neonatal fever has high SBI/meningitis risk even if well-appearing.

regimens.display_name: Fever without source, infant 1-3 months, high risk
regimens.disease_name: Fever without source, infant 1-3 months, high risk
regimens.key_clue: Age 1-3 mo with fever and any high-risk criterion: toxic, abnormal exam, WBC outside 5-15k, band >1500, UA WBC ≥5/HPF, stool WBC ≥5/HPF, prematurity/comorbidity
regimens.use_when: Any 1-3 mo infant not meeting low-risk criteria
regimens.verification_status: Thai Pediatric Acute Febrile Illness CPG 2019
regimens.manual_review_note: SOURCE_VERIFIED_ER_GATE
regimens.source_file: Needs in-person eval today/admit + young infant high-risk FWS. Do not use oral antibiotic as empirical SBI treatment.

regimens.display_name: Fever without source, infant 1-3 months, low risk
regimens.disease_name: Fever without source, infant 1-3 months, low risk
regimens.key_clue: Age 1-3 mo, fever >38°C, non-toxic, full-term/healthy, normal exam, CBC/UA low-risk
regimens.use_when: Only if all Rochester/AAP low-risk criteria met and reliable 24h follow-up
regimens.manual_review_note: GI upset, rash

regimens.display_name: Fever without source, 3-36 months, non-toxic, fever ≥39°C
regimens.disease_name: Fever without source, 3-36 months, non-toxic, fever ≥39°C
regimens.key_clue: Age 3-36 mo, non-toxic but fever ≥39°C without clear source
regimens.use_when: Needs CBC/UA risk stratification ± cultures; possible outpatient ceftriaxone if high-risk and follow-up reliable
regimens.verification_status: Thai Pediatric Acute Febrile Illness CPG 2019
regimens.manual_review_note: SOURCE_VERIFIED_GATE_PRODUCT_GAP
regimens.source_file: Do not give oral amoxicillin/azithromycin just for unexplained fever. Needs 24h follow-up if treated as outpatient. ER/admit if toxic, poor perfusion, dehydration, petechiae/purpura, altered mental status, respiratory distress, or unreliable follow-up.

regimens.display_name: Fever without source, 3-36 months, non-toxic, fever <39°C
regimens.disease_name: Fever without source, 3-36 months, non-toxic, fever <39°C
regimens.key_clue: Age 3-36 mo, non-toxic, feeding/playing, fever <39°C, no local bacterial focus
regimens.use_when: Stable child with low fever and no source
regimens.manual_review_note: GI upset, rash

regimens.display_name: Fever without source, 3-36 months, toxic appearance
regimens.disease_name: Fever without source, 3-36 months, toxic appearance
regimens.key_clue: Toxic appearance/Yale score >10, poor perfusion, lethargy, poor response, dehydration, abnormal color
regimens.use_when: Any toxic-appearing febrile child 3-36 mo, regardless WBC
regimens.verification_status: Thai Pediatric Acute Febrile Illness CPG 2019
regimens.manual_review_note: SOURCE_VERIFIED_ER_GATE
regimens.source_file: ER/admit now + toxic febrile child/SBI risk. Do not manage with oral antibiotic alone.

regimens.key_clue: Mucopurulent discharge, urethral pus, high PMN, STI risk, Gram-negative diplococci
regimens.manual_review_note: Ceftriaxone not in workbook; keep preferred regimen manual.

regimens.key_clue: Pregnant with DM risk or abnormal glucose; 24–28 week screening window
regimens.manual_review_note: Do not auto-start routine nonpregnant T2DM regimen.

regimens.key_clue: Typical heartburn/regurgitation without alarm features
regimens.manual_review_note: PPI is cornerstone; alginate add-on supported. Keep short course for simple OPD unless chronic/severe indication.

regimens.key_clue: Abnormal discharge; reproductive-age woman; cause unclear
regimens.manual_review_note: Default triage row; not a drug-only row.

regimens.key_clue: Bristol stool <3, hard stool, painful straining
regimens.manual_review_note: T.Man-Lax contains stimulant + stool-softener; use as short-course/rescue, not routine long-term.

regimens.key_clue: Heavy cyclic bleeding, stable vitals, pregnancy excluded, no red flags
regimens.manual_review_note: Tranexamic dose not in workbook; keep manual.

regimens.key_clue: HTN with CKD, especially albuminuria ≥300 mg/day or ≥300 mg/g Cr
regimens.manual_review_note: Need UACR/eGFR fields for better engine logic.

regimens.key_clue: Very high BP, often >180/110, with or without symptoms
regimens.manual_review_note: Do not label all high BP as “urgency”; use PSH concept.

regimens.key_clue: Severe BP rise with acute target-organ damage symptoms/signs
regimens.manual_review_note: Workbook oral products not sufficient for emergency protocol.

regimens.key_clue: Confirmed office BP 140–159/90–99, no CVD/CKD/TOD, low risk
regimens.manual_review_note: Price blank if workbook price blank.

regimens.key_clue: Confirmed BP ≥160/100 or grade 1 with CVD/CKD/TOD/high CV risk
regimens.manual_review_note: Check baseline comorbidities before choosing class.

regimens.key_clue: Office BP elevated; diagnosis not yet confirmed
regimens.manual_review_note: Use as triage row before chronic HTN RX.

regimens.key_clue: Pregnant, missed period, or reproductive-age patient with possible pregnancy
regimens.manual_review_note: Workbook lacks methyldopa/labetalol and IV emergency products.

regimens.key_clue: CKD with urine albumin ≥300 mg/day or ≥300 mg/g creatinine
regimens.manual_review_note: Albuminuria <300 may use any class; albuminuria ≥300 prefers ACEI/ARB.

regimens.key_clue: HT with DM; higher CV/renal risk
regimens.manual_review_note: Target differs by age; 18–65 often 120–130/70–79 if tolerated, older 130–139/70–79.

regimens.display_name: Essential hypertension grade 1 with high CV risk
regimens.disease_name: Essential hypertension grade 1 with high CV risk
regimens.key_clue: Grade 1 BP plus DM, CKD, CVD, TOD, or Thai CV risk >10%
regimens.manual_review_note: Use patient-specific comorbidity/contraindication selection.

regimens.key_clue: Office BP 140–159/90–99, no CVD/DM/CKD/TOD/high CV risk
regimens.manual_review_note: Default row for low-risk grade 1; drug only after persistence/diagnosis.

regimens.key_clue: Office BP 160–179/100–109 after proper measurement/diagnosis
regimens.manual_review_note: Combination therapy should be individualized; Exforge is workbook product but not automatic for all.

regimens.key_clue: Office BP ≥180 and/or ≥110
regimens.manual_review_note: Not default RX NOW; this is a gate row.

regimens.key_clue: Pregnant or possible pregnancy with high BP
regimens.manual_review_note: Do not auto-prescribe ACEI/ARB/DRI in pregnancy or possible pregnancy.

regimens.key_clue: Known HT with multiple CV risk factors or Thai CV Risk Score ≥10%
regimens.manual_review_note: Risk-reduction row, not HT BP-control row.

regimens.key_clue: Single high office BP or home BP concern; no emergency symptom
regimens.manual_review_note: Screening row; not an automatic drug row.

regimens.key_clue: Constipation + abdominal discomfort/pain related to defecation or stool frequency/form change
regimens.manual_review_note: Thai constipation guideline covers constipation component and IBS-C overlap, but not full IBS pain regimen.

regimens.key_clue: Persistent ≥10 d without improvement OR severe fever ≥39°C + purulent discharge/facial pain ≥3–4 d OR double-sickening after viral URI
regimens.use_when: Use when ABRS criteria met; otherwise route to viral URI/common cold
regimens.manual_review_note: diarrhea, nausea, rash

regimens.key_clue: Persistent ≥10 d without improvement OR severe fever ≥39°C + purulent discharge/facial pain ≥3–4 d OR double-sickening
regimens.use_when: Use pediatric ABRS row only when criteria met; otherwise viral URI/common cold pathway
regimens.manual_review_note: diarrhea, rash

regimens.display_name: Acute bloody diarrhea / inflammatory diarrhea evaluation
regimens.disease_name: Acute bloody diarrhea / inflammatory diarrhea evaluation
regimens.key_clue: Bloody or mucoid diarrhea, fever, severe cramps/tenderness, or sepsis concern
regimens.use_when: Use when stool is bloody/mucoid or inflammatory; routine empiric antibiotic blocked unless IDSA exception
regimens.manual_review_note: bloating

regimens.key_clue: Acute watery non-bloody diarrhea, mild cramps/nausea, no fever/sepsis/dehydration
regimens.use_when: Simple acute watery diarrhea outpatient case; no recent high-risk travel; immunocompetent; able to drink
regimens.manual_review_note: bloating

regimens.key_clue: Mild watery diarrhea / nausea / GI upset without dysentery
regimens.use_when: Mild uncomplicated viral gastroenteritis or loose stool outpatient case; able to drink; no red flags
regimens.manual_review_note: bloating

regimens.key_clue: Mild child watery diarrhea/gastroenteritis without dysentery or dehydration
regimens.use_when: Mild pediatric gastroenteritis outpatient support; child alert and able to drink; verify age/weight
regimens.manual_review_note: bloating

regimens.display_name: Group A streptococcal pharyngitis / streptococcal tonsillitis
regimens.disease_name: Group A streptococcal pharyngitis / streptococcal tonsillitis
regimens.key_clue: Sore throat with Centor/McIsaac features: fever, tonsillar swelling/exudate, tender anterior cervical nodes, absence of cough; viral symptoms lower probability
regimens.use_when: Use scoring system before RADT/NAAT/culture; test if intermediate/high probability or high-risk exposure/history; treat only if RADT/culture/NAAT positive
regimens.manual_review_note: diarrhea, rash

regimens.key_clue: Age ≥3 yr with sore throat and McIsaac/Centor features; age 3–14 adds McIsaac point; cough/coryza suggests viral
regimens.use_when: Use clinical scoring to decide testing in children ≥3 yr; does not apply to <3 yr; treat only positive RADT/culture/NAAT; backup culture if negative RADT in symptomatic child >3 yr
regimens.manual_review_note: diarrhea, rash

regimens.display_name: Pediatric community-acquired pneumonia with parapneumonic effusion / empyema
regimens.disease_name: Pediatric community-acquired pneumonia with parapneumonic effusion / empyema
regimens.key_clue: Fever/cough/pneumonia with pleuritic pain, respiratory distress, reduced breath sound, CXR effusion, toxic appearance, or persistent fever despite treatment
regimens.use_when: Use for children age 3 months–18 years when pneumonia has suspected/confirmed pleural effusion or empyema; routine OPD-only prescribing should be blocked

regimens.red_flags_one_line: In-person eval if haematuria, recurrent UTI, retention, pelvic mass/prolapse, pain, neurological deficit.
regimens.notes_internal: NG123: bladder training ≥6 weeks first-line; antimuscarinic after cognitive/contraindication review.

lines.order_text: bladder training for at least 6 weeks; urgency suppression strategies
lines.blocked_reason: source evidence link; workbook-supported BDS
lines.next_action: manual review before Fast RX NOW

lines.order_text: review caffeine, fluid intake, constipation, weight, meds/diuretics
lines.quick_caution: check urine dip/culture if UTI symptoms
lines.blocked_reason: source evidence link; workbook-supported BDS
lines.next_action: manual review before Fast RX NOW

lines.order_text: consider only if bladder training insufficient and no contraindications
lines.quick_caution: avoid narrow-angle glaucoma/retention; older adult cognition gate
lines.blocked_reason: source evidence link; workbook-supported BDS
lines.next_action: manual review before Fast RX NOW

regimens.display_name: Occult bacteremia / positive blood culture follow-up gate
regimens.disease_name: Occult bacteremia / positive blood culture follow-up gate
regimens.key_clue: Positive blood culture after outpatient fever workup
regimens.use_when: Blood culture positive in FWS child; manage by organism and clinical status
regimens.verification_status: Thai Pediatric Acute Febrile Illness CPG 2019
regimens.manual_review_note: SOURCE_VERIFIED_GATE
regimens.source_file: Needs same-day reassessment. Admit if true pathogen other than uncomplicated improving pneumococcus, persistent fever, toxic, age <1 yr with bacteremia, or meningitis concern.

regimens.key_clue: Confirmed fungus; ≤50% nail area, ≤3 nails, no matrix/proximal involvement, superficial white type
regimens.manual_review_note: Amorolfine nail lacquer and 40% urea not found in workbook.

regimens.key_clue: Confirmed fungus with >50% nail area, >3 nails, proximal/matrix involvement, nail thickness >2 mm, dermatophytoma, or skin spread
regimens.manual_review_note: Terbinafine and amorolfine missing; itraconazole/fluconazole available but require careful interaction/lab monitoring.

regimens.key_clue: Discolored/thickened/brittle nail, onycholysis, subungual debris, usually toenail
regimens.manual_review_note: Antifungal stewardship: lab confirmation required before systemic antifungal.

regimens.display_name: OSA/snoring with nasal obstruction or rhinitis
regimens.disease_name: OSA/snoring with nasal obstruction or rhinitis
regimens.key_clue: OSA/snoring plus blocked nose, allergic rhinitis, dry nose, poor PAP tolerance due nasal symptoms
regimens.manual_review_note: Nasal drugs may improve obstruction/PAP tolerance but are not definitive OSA therapy. Limit topical decongestant duration.

regimens.display_name: PAP intolerance / poor response in adult OSA
regimens.disease_name: PAP intolerance / poor response in adult OSA
regimens.key_clue: Cannot tolerate PAP, symptoms not improved, high residual AHI/leak, side effects, or refuses PAP
regimens.manual_review_note: Poor response means not tolerated, symptoms not improved/worse, significant side effects, or refusal after counseling.

regimens.display_name: Atypical acute wheeze gate in child
regimens.disease_name: Atypical acute wheeze gate in child
regimens.key_clue: First severe wheeze, focal signs, choking, no URI, recurrent/persistent, congenital/cardiac/aspiration clues
regimens.use_when: Wheeze that does not fit simple viral-induced wheeze/bronchiolitis
regimens.verification_status: Thai Pediatric Acute Respiratory Infection CPG 2562
regimens.manual_review_note: SOURCE_VERIFIED_SAFETY_GATE
regimens.source_file: Needs in-person eval today or ER depending severity.

regimens.key_clue: Infant/toddler, URI prodrome, wheeze/crackles, increased WOB, usually viral
regimens.use_when: Typical bronchiolitis without severe distress or hypoxia
regimens.manual_review_note: local irritation

regimens.display_name: Acute bronchitis / acute tracheobronchitis in child
regimens.disease_name: Acute bronchitis / acute tracheobronchitis in child
regimens.key_clue: Acute cough after URI, ± low fever/rhinorrhea, no tachypnea/chest indrawing, no pneumonia signs
regimens.use_when: Uncomplicated acute cough/bronchitis, likely viral, stable child
regimens.manual_review_note: GI upset, rash

regimens.key_clue: Wheeze where asthma/reactive airway cannot be excluded
regimens.use_when: Use only as monitored trial; continue only if clear response
regimens.manual_review_note: product gap

regimens.key_clue: Profuse rice-water watery diarrhea, outbreak/travel risk, rapid dehydration
regimens.use_when: Suspected cholera or outbreak-compatible severe watery diarrhea
regimens.manual_review_note: Antibiotic should not delay rehydration.
regimens.source_file: Thai Pediatric Acute Diarrhea CPG 2562 + workbook

regimens.key_clue: Barking cough, hoarseness, inspiratory stridor, often worse at night
regimens.use_when: Mild-moderate croup without toxic appearance or severe obstruction
regimens.manual_review_note: product gap

regimens.display_name: Severe viral croup / upper airway obstruction gate
regimens.disease_name: Severe viral croup / upper airway obstruction gate
regimens.key_clue: Stridor at rest, severe retraction, agitation/lethargy, cyanosis, poor air entry
regimens.use_when: Severe croup or concern for airway obstruction
regimens.verification_status: Thai Pediatric Acute Respiratory Infection CPG 2562
regimens.manual_review_note: SOURCE_VERIFIED_ER_GATE
regimens.source_file: ER now + upper airway obstruction risk.

regimens.key_clue: Thirsty, dry mouth, reduced tears/urine, mild/moderate dehydration; no shock
regimens.use_when: Mild/moderate dehydration, able to drink ORS
regimens.manual_review_note: bloating

regimens.key_clue: Watery stool, clinically hydrated, active child, tolerates PO
regimens.use_when: No dehydration and no blood/high-risk features
regimens.manual_review_note: bloating

regimens.display_name: Severe dehydration / shock from acute diarrhea
regimens.disease_name: Severe dehydration / shock from acute diarrhea
regimens.key_clue: Shock, lethargy/unconscious, unable to drink, abnormal breathing, poor perfusion, severe dehydration
regimens.use_when: Any child diarrhea with severe dehydration or shock
regimens.manual_review_note: No OPD prescription row.

regimens.key_clue: Vomiting limiting ORS, likely AGE, no bilious vomiting/surgical abdomen
regimens.use_when: Vomiting with diarrhea, age ≥6 mo, not severe dehydration
regimens.manual_review_note: bloating

regimens.display_name: Acute dysentery / invasive diarrhea in child
regimens.disease_name: Acute dysentery / invasive diarrhea in child
regimens.key_clue: Bloody/mucus stool, high fever, tenesmus, stool WBC/RBC, abdominal pain
regimens.use_when: Invasive diarrhea pattern or visible blood/mucus stool
regimens.manual_review_note: bloating

regimens.key_clue: High fever, severe sore throat, drooling, dysphagia, tripod, muffled voice, toxic, little/no barking cough
regimens.use_when: Any suspected epiglottitis
regimens.verification_status: Thai Pediatric Acute Respiratory Infection CPG 2562
regimens.manual_review_note: SOURCE_VERIFIED_ER_GATE
regimens.source_file: ER now + possible fatal airway obstruction. Avoid tongue depressor/throat manipulation outside airway-ready setting.

regimens.key_clue: Influenza-like illness with pneumonia or high-risk child
regimens.use_when: Suspected influenza pneumonia or influenza with risk for complication
regimens.manual_review_note: product gap

regimens.key_clue: Prominent dry cough, less toxic, school-age, outbreak, extrapulmonary signs, poor beta-lactam response
regimens.use_when: Suspected Mycoplasma/Chlamydophila pneumoniae or failure of amoxicillin
regimens.manual_review_note: GI upset, QT caution

regimens.key_clue: Fever + cough + tachypnea/crepitation but no hypoxia/severe distress
regimens.use_when: Stable outpatient pneumonia when bacterial pneumonia is suspected
regimens.manual_review_note: GI upset, rash

regimens.display_name: Severe pneumonia / complicated pneumonia gate, child
regimens.disease_name: Severe pneumonia / complicated pneumonia gate, child
regimens.key_clue: Respiratory distress, SpO2 <92%, grunting/apnea, cyanosis, shock, dehydration, complication
regimens.use_when: Any severe pneumonia sign or outpatient failure after 48 hr
regimens.verification_status: Thai Pediatric Acute Respiratory Infection CPG 2562
regimens.manual_review_note: SOURCE_VERIFIED_ER_GATE

regimens.key_clue: Prolonged diarrhea, exposure risk, bloating/greasy stool for Giardia; dysentery for amebiasis
regimens.use_when: Symptomatic or confirmed Giardia/E. histolytica; not routine viral diarrhea
regimens.manual_review_note: bloating

regimens.display_name: Recurrent viral-induced wheeze / preschool asthma assessment gate
regimens.disease_name: Recurrent viral-induced wheeze / preschool asthma assessment gate
regimens.key_clue: ≥2 wheeze episodes or ≥3/year, frequent bronchodilator need, severe episodes requiring steroid/admission
regimens.use_when: Follow-up/long-term controller assessment, not acute RX
regimens.verification_status: Thai Pediatric Acute Respiratory Infection CPG 2562
regimens.manual_review_note: SOURCE_VERIFIED_FOLLOWUP_GATE
regimens.source_file: Needs specialist/close follow-up if recurrent severe, poor growth, chronic symptoms between episodes, focal chest signs, clubbing, aspiration history, congenital disease suspicion, or recurrent pneumonia.

regimens.key_clue: Toxic croup-like illness, high fever, thick purulent secretion, poor response to nebulized epinephrine
regimens.use_when: Suspected bacterial tracheitis
regimens.verification_status: Thai Pediatric Acute Respiratory Infection CPG 2562
regimens.manual_review_note: SOURCE_VERIFIED_ER_GATE
regimens.source_file: ER now + airway obstruction/toxic shock risk.

regimens.key_clue: Known urinary tract anomaly, obstruction, renal dysfunction, immunocompromise, severe illness
regimens.use_when: Any UTI with complicated features
regimens.manual_review_note: Product gap for IV antibiotics.

regimens.display_name: Lower urinary tract infection / cystitis in child
regimens.disease_name: Lower urinary tract infection / cystitis in child
regimens.key_clue: Dysuria, frequency, urgency, suprapubic pain, afebrile or no systemic signs
regimens.use_when: Child 2 mo–5 yr with lower UTI pattern, stable, able PO; urine test/culture preferred
regimens.manual_review_note: GI upset, rash

regimens.key_clue: Upper UTI ≥2 times, or upper UTI once plus lower UTI, or lower UTI ≥3 times
regimens.use_when: Recurrent pattern or relapse shortly after treatment
regimens.manual_review_note: Add bladder-bowel dysfunction questionnaire fields later.

regimens.display_name: Febrile UTI / suspected acute pyelonephritis in child
regimens.disease_name: Febrile UTI / suspected acute pyelonephritis in child
regimens.key_clue: Fever/high fever ± vomiting/flank/CVA tenderness; urinary symptoms may be absent in young child
regimens.use_when: Suspected upper UTI/febrile UTI, stable and able PO; requires urine testing/culture
regimens.manual_review_note: GI upset, rash

regimens.key_clue: Acute wheeze with viral URI; improves with bronchodilator; not classic bronchiolitis
regimens.use_when: Child ≤5 yr with viral URI + wheeze, stable, no severe signs
regimens.manual_review_note: product gap

regimens.key_clue: Confirmed pemphigus; BSA <5% and/or mucosal disease not affecting swallowing/analgesic need; PDAI ≤15
regimens.manual_review_note: Prednisolone, topical clobetasol/triamcinolone oral gel, AZA, MMF, rituximab unavailable in workbook.

regimens.key_clue: BSA >5%, PDAI >15, severe oral/mucosal disease, dysphagia, dehydration, infection risk, extensive erosions
regimens.manual_review_note: Do not show routine OPD RX; high-risk immunosuppression requires labs and monitoring.

regimens.key_clue: Flaccid blister, easy rupture, painful erosion, oral/genital/ocular mucosal erosion
regimens.manual_review_note: Workbook lacks core pemphigus systemic and topical steroid/immunosuppressive products.

regimens.key_clue: Fever with clear viral features or low-risk child, non-toxic, feeding/playing acceptable
regimens.use_when: Use when child is stable, no SBI red flags, no local bacterial focus
regimens.manual_review_note: GI upset, rash

regimens.display_name: Posterior canal benign paroxysmal positional vertigo
regimens.disease_name: Posterior canal benign paroxysmal positional vertigo
regimens.key_clue: Dix-Hallpike provokes vertigo with torsional upbeating nystagmus, latency, resolves within 60 sec
regimens.manual_review_note: Epley/CRP is main treatment. Dimenhydrinate is optional rescue only and should not replace maneuver.

regimens.key_clue: Troublesome reflux symptoms despite ≥8 weeks standard-dose PPI
regimens.manual_review_note: Refractory means symptoms, not always true reflux. Do not keep escalating PPI indefinitely without reassessment.

regimens.key_clue: FPG 100–125 or 2h-OGTT 140–199; no overt DM
regimens.manual_review_note: Default no drug.

regimens.key_clue: Cramping lower abdominal pain just before/after menses; worst with heavy flow; normal pelvic history; no secondary red flags
regimens.manual_review_note: NSAIDs first-line if no contraindication; COC is option especially if contraception desired.

regimens.key_clue: Age ≥35, LDL-C <190, Thai CV Risk Score ≥10%, no ASCVD/DM/CKD
regimens.manual_review_note: Use Thai CV Risk Score gate before showing RX NOW.

regimens.key_clue: Still symptomatic after adequate lifestyle + conventional laxatives with good compliance
regimens.manual_review_note: New agents not proven superior to conventional laxatives and are cost/availability dependent.

regimens.key_clue: Still sleepy after apparently adequate PAP use and residual AHI controlled
regimens.manual_review_note: Modafinil/armodafinil not recommended as routine Thai guideline patch; specialist-only if truly residual sleepiness after adequate PAP.

regimens.key_clue: Uncontrolled BP despite lifestyle + 3 appropriate antihypertensives including diuretic
regimens.manual_review_note: Resistant HT row should not be first-line.

regimens.key_clue: New/worsening dysmenorrhea after years of painless menses; pelvic pathology symptoms
regimens.manual_review_note: Investigation-first row.

regimens.key_clue: TG ≥500 mg/dL; pancreatitis risk
regimens.manual_review_note: Needs TG repeat and renal/liver monitoring.

regimens.key_clue: Loud snoring but low OSA risk; STOP-Bang <3 and no strong clinical/comorbidity concern
regimens.manual_review_note: This is not a drug-first disease. Avoid sedatives for sleep/snoring.

regimens.display_name: Sudden sensorineural hearing loss / idiopathic sudden hearing loss
regimens.disease_name: Sudden sensorineural hearing loss / idiopathic sudden hearing loss
regimens.key_clue: Sudden unilateral hearing loss within 72h ± tinnitus/vertigo; normal otoscopy or no conductive cause
regimens.manual_review_note: Need real oral steroid product/BDS/price before active RX; guideline is adult ≥18, no pediatric regimen from this source

regimens.key_clue: Loud habitual snoring + witnessed apnea/gasping/daytime sleepiness or STOP-Bang ≥3 or major comorbidity
regimens.manual_review_note: Do not prescribe stimulant or sleeping pills from short OPD input. Diagnosis requires sleep evaluation/testing.

regimens.key_clue: Adult T2DM, stable OPD, not pregnant, no DKA/HHS, no severe symptoms
regimens.manual_review_note: Metformin first-line only if eGFR and contraindications allow.

regimens.display_name: Type 2 diabetes mellitus, not controlled on metformin
regimens.disease_name: Type 2 diabetes mellitus, not controlled on metformin
regimens.key_clue: Known T2DM; A1C/FPG above target despite lifestyle + metformin
regimens.manual_review_note: SU dosing should be individualized; avoid glibenclamide in elderly/CKD risk.

regimens.key_clue: FPG >300 mg/dL or A1C >11% or symptomatic hyperglycemia
regimens.manual_review_note: Do not force oral-only OPD regimen.

regimens.key_clue: Tinnitus ≥6 months causing sleep, concentration, mood, communication, or quality-of-life impairment
regimens.manual_review_note: Medication is not RX NOW. CBT is recommended; sound therapy is optional; hearing aids if hearing loss.

regimens.key_clue: Ringing/buzzing/roaring/clicking sound without external source; first exclude secondary cause
regimens.manual_review_note: Do not use betahistine, dimenhydrinate, amitriptyline, gabapentin, pregabalin, benzodiazepines, intratympanic steroids, Ginkgo, melatonin, zinc, or vitamins as routine primary tinnitus treatment.

regimens.display_name: Unilateral or pulsatile tinnitus / possible secondary tinnitus
regimens.disease_name: Unilateral or pulsatile tinnitus / possible secondary tinnitus
regimens.key_clue: One-sided tinnitus, pulse-synchronous tinnitus, asymmetric hearing loss, focal neurologic sign, or objective tinnitus
regimens.manual_review_note: Keep separate from primary tinnitus. Imaging is not routine; it is red-flag/secondary-cause driven.

regimens.key_clue: Yellow-green frothy discharge, vaginal erythema, strawberry cervix, motile trichomonad, pH >4.5
regimens.manual_review_note: Follow-up wet smear at 7 days and 3 months per source.

regimens.key_clue: Widespread severe wheals/itch, no airway compromise, no hypotension, no systemic anaphylaxis
regimens.use_when: Severe acute urticaria flare after red flags excluded
regimens.manual_review_note: drowsy, dry mouth

regimens.key_clue: Itch/burning, curd-like discharge, pH usually <4.5; no strong STI signs
regimens.manual_review_note: Use vaginal clotrimazole in pregnancy/lactation instead of oral azole.

regimens.key_clue: ≥4 symptomatic VC episodes/year or persistent/recurrent symptoms
regimens.manual_review_note: Do not auto-dispense 6-month regimen without clinician review.

regimens.key_clue: Office BP ≥140/90 but home BP <135/85
regimens.manual_review_note: Monitoring/risk row, not routine RX.
